jokes.text: They said it couldn't be done, but here we are, stealing an unlimited supply of birthday grade helium from the unsuspected moon. Um... sun.
jokes.setup: Bender confidently declares they're stealing from the moon
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual correction that it's actually the sun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender overconfidence paired with Fry's matter-of-fact correction. Economy is excellent - maximum confusion in minimal words.

jokes.text: At night it's called the moon.
jokes.setup: Fry corrects that they're on the sun, not the moon
jokes.punchline: Bender's confident but completely wrong astronomical explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic delivered with Bender's trademark misplaced confidence. The economy is perfect - maximum stupid in minimum words.

jokes.text: Mine collapse! Mine collapse! So? Mine collapse, too.
jokes.setup: Desperate miner screaming about the mine collapsing
jokes.punchline: Bender misinterprets 'mine' as possessive pronoun, casually relates
jokes.explanation: Classic wordplay with perfect timing. Bender's casual response to a life-or-death situation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Won't that sear our tender flesh, sealing in the juices?
jokes.setup: Discussion of 10,000 degree plasma burning them
jokes.punchline: Fry describes being cooked like meat, focusing on juice retention
jokes.explanation: Dark cooking imagery applied to their potential death is morbidly funny. The culinary focus on 'sealing in juices' is unexpectedly specific.

jokes.text: You clearly weren't paying attention to what I'm about to say.
jokes.setup: Professor dismisses their concerns about being burned alive
jokes.punchline: Logic loop - accusing them of not paying attention to future speech
jokes.explanation: Brilliant temporal paradox joke. The Professor's senile logic creates perfect absurdist humor with incredible economy.

jokes.text: Guess what. Hell is real. Oh, good, it works. Toodle-oo!
jokes.setup: Professor tests heat spray by dunking matter in lava
jokes.punchline: Casually announces Hell's existence, then cheerfully dismisses it
jokes.explanation: Dark theological revelation treated as minor lab observation. The Professor's casual 'Toodle-oo!' after confirming Hell exists is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Stop laughing. Help is on the way! In the meantime, you should sing a song to keep your spirits up.
jokes.setup: Miners are in agonizing pain from helium exposure
jokes.punchline: Leela cheerfully suggests singing while they're suffering
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from Leela's tone-deaf optimism. The helium voice effect adds physical comedy layer.

jokes.text: I'll stay here and be in charge of not dying.
jokes.setup: Team prepares for dangerous rescue mission
jokes.punchline: Hermes assigns himself the bureaucratic role of survival
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes bureaucratic cowardice. Treating self-preservation as an official job assignment is great character work.

jokes.text: Just one more fire between me and a well-earned nap.
jokes.setup: Bender is running through dangerous solar plasma
jokes.punchline: Treats life-threatening rescue as minor inconvenience before naptime
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender selfishness - reducing heroic rescue to obstacle between him and sleep. Good character integration.

jokes.text: The heroic robot, with no concern for his own safety, has bravely charged back into the mine. What else is on?
jokes.setup: Linda dramatically narrates Bender's heroic rescue efforts
jokes.punchline: Morbo immediately gets bored and channel surfs
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Linda's dramatic reporting and Morbo's alien indifference to human heroism.

jokes.text: I am? Huh, I always strongly suspected I was one of those.
jokes.setup: Miner calls Bender a true hero
jokes.punchline: Bender acts surprised but claims he always suspected his heroism
jokes.explanation: Bender's false modesty covering massive ego is classic character work. The phrasing 'one of those' is nicely vague.

jokes.text: An actress? A celebrity chef? Stay tuned for the next 14 rescues. 14?
jokes.setup: Reporter promises more dramatic rescues coming up
jokes.punchline: Bender realizes he has to do 14 more rescues, sounds exhausted
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with Bender's dawning realization of the work ahead. His deflated reaction sells it.

jokes.text: Morbo is not weeping. He has a... a parasite in his eye.
jokes.setup: Emotional rescue scene where everyone made it out alive
jokes.punchline: Morbo tries to maintain alien superiority while clearly crying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Morbo character moment - his alien pride conflicting with unexpected human emotion. The parasite excuse is absurdly specific.

jokes.text: But we only have the one medal. So please direct all your admiration to the main guy-- Bender! Now, you two step aside.
jokes.setup: Ceremony to honor the heroic Planet Express crew
jokes.punchline: Arbitrarily gives all credit to Bender, dismisses the others
jokes.explanation: Decent bureaucratic arbitrariness joke. The casual dismissal of the actual team is appropriately unfair.

jokes.text: My baby's in that podium! Somebody call the fire department! There is no fire department. I sold it to pay for Bender's medal.
jokes.setup: Fire breaks out at the ceremony, woman screams for fire department
jokes.punchline: Mayor reveals he sold the fire department to afford Bender's medal
jokes.explanation: Great escalating irony - the medal ceremony directly caused the emergency they can't handle. Good governmental incompetence satire.

jokes.text: These boys must have hero in their bones. And you, ma'am, must have heroine in your veins.
jokes.setup: Nixon praising the crew's heroic rescue of the woman's baby
jokes.punchline: Accidentally implies the woman is a drug addict via hero/heroine wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with dark implications. Nixon's oblivious delivery of the drug reference makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Bite my shiny metal axe!
jokes.setup: Bender has been appointed as firefighter
jokes.punchline: Adapts his signature catchphrase to his new firefighting role
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that adapts the familiar catchphrase to the situation. Not groundbreaking but satisfying for fans.

jokes.text: Zoidberg, quit turning us on and go polish your nozzle.
jokes.setup: Leela talking about preparing the sexy firehouse
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about Zoidberg being arousing and cleaning equipment
jokes.explanation: Basic sexual innuendo that's somewhat forced. The double meaning works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Yo! Hero in the house. I just got back from my daily medal strut.
jokes.setup: Bender returns to the firehouse
jokes.punchline: Announces himself egotistically and reveals he parades his medal around daily
jokes.explanation: Good Bender ego display. 'Medal strut' is a nice specific detail that shows his vanity.

jokes.text: The locations form a perfect shape of some kind. No! Bender was at every one of those places right before the fire started.
jokes.setup: Amy notices the fire locations form a perfect shape
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately jumps to the correct but more obvious conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where the mathematical pattern is less important than the obvious suspect. Amy's overthinking vs Leela's directness.

jokes.text: Oh, I don't know. Possible, feasible. I don't know. Seems like maybe. Who knows? Maybe I do. Yeah, probably.
jokes.setup: Question of whether Bender could be an arsonist
jokes.punchline: Crew goes from uncertainty to gradual acceptance in overlapping dialogue
jokes.explanation: Good escalating group consensus building. The overlapping uncertain-to-certain progression works well.

jokes.text: He would never burn something down to get attention. For insurance money? Yes. Revenge? You bet. To give an autumn night that crisp chestnutty smell? Absolutely. But not for fame, women or thrills.
jokes.setup: Fry defending Bender against arson accusations
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly specific and absurd reasons Bender would commit arson, except for the actual reason
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating defense that becomes an indictment. The autumn smell detail is absurdly specific and poetic. Perfect Fry logic.

jokes.text: Hey, guys, it's me, Bender. The famous, sexy, thrilling hero.
jokes.setup: Fry just said Bender wouldn't burn things for fame, women or thrills
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately enters describing himself with exactly those qualities
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing. Bender unknowingly contradicts Fry's defense by describing himself exactly as Fry said he wouldn't.

jokes.text: What do you want in that, beans or just blood?
jokes.setup: Bender suggests making firehouse chili
jokes.punchline: Offers blood as the alternative to beans in chili recipe
jokes.explanation: Dark cooking humor that fits Bender's robot lack of understanding of human food. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, no! Surprisingly, a fire has broken out at the meat market where I am! We'll be there right away. Yeah, don't trouble yourselves. This is just a one-hero fire.
jokes.setup: Another convenient fire breaks out where Bender happens to be
jokes.punchline: Bender dismisses help, claiming he can handle this 'one-hero fire' alone
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's desire for solo glory. 'One-hero fire' is a nice invented term.

jokes.text: Scoop Chang, New New York Magazine, Kosher Recipes Editor, iPad Edition.
jokes.setup: Reporter introduces himself to interview Bender
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and irrelevant credentials ending with technology specification
jokes.explanation: Decent escalating absurdity in the reporter's overly specific job title. The kosher recipes to iPad specification is a nice non sequitur.

jokes.text: Well, I was shoplifting a lamp chop when suddenly fire erupted from the liver tray.
jokes.setup: Woman describes what happened during the fire
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to shoplifting while describing the emergency
jokes.explanation: Good incidental character moment. The casual admission of shoplifting during an emergency interview is nicely oblivious.

jokes.text: We can have four idiots and a fat guy, but no arsonists.
jokes.setup: Leela explaining why Bender can't be in the fire department
jokes.punchline: Casual insult to the entire crew while drawing the line at arsonists
jokes.explanation: Good cutting insult that establishes standards. The specific count and casual brutality work well.

jokes.text: How could a fire learn to talk? Did you burn up a dictionary? Or a parrot?
jokes.setup: Flame creature starts talking to Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's robot logic tries to explain talking fire through consumed objects
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist robot logic. Bender's attempt to rationally explain the supernatural through consumption is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: A billion years ago, I attempted to overthrow the democratically elected alderman from my district.
jokes.setup: Flame being explains its ancient evil origin story
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was just local municipal politics gone wrong
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anticlimax - cosmic evil reduced to petty local politics. The bureaucratic specificity is perfect.

jokes.text: Get to the point, Flame-O.
jokes.setup: Ancient flame being giving dramatic backstory
jokes.punchline: Bender impatiently gives it a dismissive nickname and rushes the exposition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender impatience with dramatic exposition. 'Flame-O' is a great dismissive nickname that shows his lack of respect.

jokes.text: You're huffin' for a snuffin', boy!
jokes.setup: Flame being threatens to destroy the planet
jokes.punchline: Bender responds with tough-guy slang using fire terminology
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay threat using fire-extinguishing terminology. Fits Bender's tough-guy persona while being thematically appropriate.

jokes.text: Once I make my way there, your planet will go micro-nova and become a miniature sun, with me as its Supreme Alderman.
jokes.setup: Flame being describes its plan to reach Earth's core
jokes.punchline: Cosmic destruction described as municipal appointment
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the alderman backstory. Reducing planetary destruction to local political office is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Sooner or later, a polar bear will happen by. A nice, flammable polar bear. Polar bears don't burn. I've tried many times.
jokes.setup: Flame being threatens eventual escape via polar bear
jokes.punchline: Bender casually reveals he's tried burning polar bears multiple times
jokes.explanation: Dark casual revelation that perfectly fits Bender's character. The matter-of-fact delivery of repeated polar bear arson attempts is brilliantly disturbing.

jokes.text: No, free. But it's got ads on it. That seems fair.
jokes.setup: Bender asks if the Find My Robot app cost 99 cents
jokes.punchline: Fry's acceptance that ads are a fair trade for free apps
jokes.explanation: Decent modern tech observation that feels natural in character. Not groundbreaking but relatable.

jokes.text: I know you would never set a fire that would endanger your own valuables.
jokes.setup: Fry explains why he believes Bender is innocent
jokes.punchline: His faith is based on Bender's selfishness, not heroism
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight - Fry knows Bender well enough to trust his selfishness over his heroism. Perfect friendship logic.

jokes.text: That's not a flame creature. It's your pilot light.
jokes.setup: Bender shows Fry the 'flame creature from the sun'
jokes.punchline: Fry immediately identifies it as Bender's mundane pilot light
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Bender's grand delusion. The economy is perfect - one line destroys the entire elaborate fantasy.

jokes.text: Stupid Bender. Liar, liar, pants on fire.
jokes.setup: Fry realizes Bender has been lying about the flame creature
jokes.punchline: Uses fire-themed children's rhyme to insult the guy who's been setting fires
jokes.explanation: Decent thematic wordplay using fire imagery for the arsonist. Not particularly clever but thematically appropriate.

jokes.text: It's like the flame has a mind of its own! It's deaky! Freaky deaky!
jokes.setup: Fire is spreading in unusual ways
jokes.punchline: Character gets so flustered they create nonsense words
jokes.explanation: Mild character panic moment. The made-up 'deaky' isn't particularly funny but shows appropriate confusion.

jokes.text: What kind of coffin do you want?!
jokes.setup: Leela is trying to rescue Fry from the fire
jokes.punchline: Instead of encouragement, immediately jumps to funeral planning
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor subverting rescue dialogue expectations. Leela's practical pessimism is funny.

jokes.text: All the lava in the world won't wash away your lies.
jokes.setup: Fry confronting Bender about his deception
jokes.punchline: Overwrought metaphor using lava instead of water for washing
jokes.explanation: Nice absurdist twist on the washing away sins metaphor. The lava substitution fits the setting.

jokes.text: I guess some grown men just don't like being carried.
jokes.setup: Fry protesting being rescued
jokes.punchline: Bender's matter-of-fact observation about masculine pride during life-or-death situation
jokes.explanation: Good understatement joke about masculine pride. The casual observation during emergency fits Bender's character.

jokes.text: Yes! Suck it, Gilgamesh!
jokes.setup: Mystic Aldermen declare Bender the greatest hero in Earth's history
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately taunts ancient legendary hero
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Bender immediately turns validation into competition with ancient heroes. Perfect economy and pure Bender ego.

jokes.text: Praise Gilgamesh!
jokes.setup: Amy celebrating that Fry is safe
jokes.punchline: Ironically praises the same hero Bender just insulted
jokes.explanation: Good quick callback that creates ironic contrast with Bender's earlier taunt. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I guess a welding torch is no way to catch a rat.
jokes.setup: Fry admits he started the fire
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was using a welding torch for rodent control
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity moment. Using industrial welding equipment for pest control is absurdly over-the-top and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Fry did it?! Ow! You're a horrible person! And your carelessness nearly cost the life of my best friend. You.
jokes.setup: Bender learns Fry started the fire and gets angry
jokes.punchline: Reveals his 'best friend' is himself while hitting Fry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender selfishness reveal. The audience expects him to say Fry is his best friend, but he means himself. Excellent character consistency and structure.
